chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The (saying of) ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Willing’ in the oaths

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/329)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/329)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) مَنِ اسْتَثْنَى فِي يَمِينٍ فَلَا حِنْثَ وَ لَا كَفَّارَةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who makes an exclusion in an oath, so there is neither a breaking (of the oath), nor an expiation (to pay)’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one makes the exception in taking an oath there is no violation or expiation.’”
